name: Jibrail
description: Jibrail, often anglicized as Gabriel, is a prominent archangel in the Abrahamic religions, encompassing Judaism, Christianity, and Islam. He is primarily known as the messenger of God, conveying divine revelations and instructions to prophets and individuals chosen for specific purposes. In Islamic tradition, Jibrail is considered the most important angel, entrusted with delivering the Quran to the Prophet Muhammad. His appearances are often depicted as majestic and awe-inspiring, radiating divine light and power. Beyond delivering messages, Jibrail is also associated with divine intervention, protection, and guidance. He plays a crucial role in pivotal moments in religious narratives, acting as a conduit between the celestial realm and the earthly sphere. His presence signifies divine will and the active involvement of God in human affairs. Jibrail's role transcends mere communication; he embodies divine authority and serves as a reminder of the constant connection between the divine and the mortal world.
subtitle: The Messenger of God

keyMyths.title: The Revelation of the Quran
keyMyths.description: In Islamic tradition, Jibrail played a pivotal role in the revelation of the Quran to the Prophet Muhammad. Over a period of approximately 23 years, Jibrail appeared to Muhammad in various forms, reciting the verses of the Quran and guiding him in understanding their meaning. These encounters are described as profound and transformative experiences, shaping the foundation of Islamic belief and practice. The process of revelation is a central tenet of Islam, solidifying Jibrail's status as the primary intermediary between God and humanity.

keyMyths.title: The Annunciation to Mary
keyMyths.description: In Christian theology, Gabriel (Jibrail) is the angel who appeared to Mary, the mother of Jesus, to announce that she would conceive and bear a son through the power of the Holy Spirit. This event, known as the Annunciation, is a cornerstone of Christian belief, marking the beginning of the incarnation of God in human form. Gabriel's message to Mary is one of hope, divine favor, and the fulfillment of prophecy, signifying the arrival of the Messiah. The account highlights Gabriel's role as a herald of significant events.

keyMyths.title: Gabriel's Instructions to Zechariah
keyMyths.description: In the Gospel of Luke, Gabriel appears to Zechariah, a priest, while he is serving in the temple. Gabriel announces that Zechariah's wife, Elizabeth, who is barren, will conceive and give birth to a son, John the Baptist, who will prepare the way for the Lord. Because Zechariah doubts Gabriel's message, he is struck mute until the child is born. This narrative emphasizes Gabriel's authority as a divine messenger and the consequences of questioning God's will. The story sets the stage for the birth of John the Baptist and his pivotal role in Christian salvation history.

sources.author: Wheeler, Brannon M.
sources.work: *Prophets in the Quran: An Introduction to the Quran and Muslim Exegesis*
sources.description: Examines the role of prophets, including Muhammad and the role of Jibrail in delivering the Quran.
sources.text: Examines the role of prophets, including Muhammad and the role of Jibrail in delivering the Quran.
